name: Alto de Piedrafita y Alto de Mallecina – circular desde Pravia por Salas
name_original: Alto de Piedrafita y Alto de Mallecina – circular desde Pravia por Salas
region_intro: Road cycling around Pravia, a municipality in Asturias, Spain, offers diverse terrain for cyclists. The region features a blend of coastal landscapes, fertile river valleys formed by the Nalón and Narcea rivers, and challenging inland hills. Elevation changes are a common characteristic, with routes often including significant ascents and descents through varied natural environments. The area provides a mix of well-paved roads and scenic countryside suitable for different cycling preferences. Best road cycling routes around Pravia The most popular road cycling route is Alto de Piedrafita y Alto de Mallecina – circular desde Pravia por Salas, a 40.4 miles (65.0 km) trail that takes 3 hours 15 minutes to complete. This difficult route features substantial elevation gain through the Asturian countryside. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Peñaflor Bridge – Santa María del Mar Beach loop from Pravia, a difficult 51.1 miles (82.2 km) path. This route combines river valley sections with coastal views and significant climbs. Local road cyclists also love the Alto del Praviano loop from Pravia, a 31.8 miles (51.1 km) trail leading through hilly terrain, often completed in about 2 hours 34 minutes. Road cycling around Pravia is defined by river valleys, a scenic coastline, and rolling hills, offering varied landscapes for exploration. The network provides options for both moderate and difficult routes, catering to different ability levels. The routes in Pravia are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.9 stars from 9 reviews. More than 800 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Pravia's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available in Pravia?
A: Pravia offers a wide selection of road cycling routes, with over 25 options available on komoot. These routes cater to various preferences, ranging from moderate to difficult, ensuring there's a suitable ride for many cyclists.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes around Pravia?
A: Road cycling in Pravia features diverse terrain, including scenic coastal stretches, fertile river valleys carved by the Nalón and Narcea rivers, and challenging inland hills. Expect significant elevation changes, with routes often including substantial ascents and descents through varied natural environments.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Pravia?
A: June is a particularly popular month for cyclists in Pravia, suggesting favorable weather conditions. Generally, the region's climate makes it suitable for road cycling through much of the spring, summer, and early autumn, allowing riders to enjoy the diverse landscapes.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes in Pravia?
A: Yes, many road cycling routes in Pravia are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1078412272/alto-de-piedrafita-y-alto-de-mallecina-circular-desde-pravia-por-salas" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto de Piedrafita y Alto de Mallecina – circular desde Pravia por Salas&lt;/a&gt; is a popular circular route offering challenging climbs and scenic views.

Q: Are there routes suitable for less experienced road cyclists in Pravia?
A: While Pravia is known for its challenging terrain, there are moderate routes available. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38213115" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto del Praviano loop from Pravia&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 31.8-mile (51.1 km) trail that still provides engaging hilly terrain without the extreme difficulty of some longer routes.

Q: What scenic viewpoints can I find along road cycling routes in Pravia?
A: Pravia offers numerous natural viewpoints. You can enjoy expansive panoramas from elevated areas like Monteagudo, La Peñona, and Cueto. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4999971" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vidio Lighthouse&lt;/a&gt; also provides breathtaking ocean views from dramatic cliffs.

Q: Are there any natural attractions to see while cycling in Pravia?
A: Absolutely. The region boasts stunning natural features such as the tranquil &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/93692" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Beach of Silence&lt;/a&gt; with its impressive geological formations, and the accessible &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3958024" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Playa de Aguilar&lt;/a&gt;. You might also encounter the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/865581" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Nonaya Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; on some routes.

Q: Can I visit historical or cultural sites during my road cycling trip in Pravia?
A: Yes, Pravia is rich in history. You can explore sites like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/353344" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Selgas Palace and the Jesús Nazareno Church‑Pantheon&lt;/a&gt;, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5125287" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Collegiate Church of St. Mary Major in Salas&lt;/a&gt;. The historic center of Pravia itself, with its Indiano Palaces and the Village Hall, is also worth exploring.

Q: Are there options for food and drink stops along the road cycling routes?
A: Yes, some routes incorporate stops where you can refuel. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32078382" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Cortijo Café – Alto del Praviano loop from Pravia&lt;/a&gt; specifically mentions a café, indicating opportunities for breaks and refreshments.

Q: What are the typical distances and durations for road cycling routes in Pravia?
A: Routes vary significantly. You can find rides like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38213115" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto del Praviano loop from Pravia&lt;/a&gt;, which is about 31.8 miles (51.1 km) and takes around 2 hours 34 minutes. Longer, more challenging routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32092411" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñaflor Bridge – Nalón River loop from Pravia&lt;/a&gt;, can extend to 73.5 miles (118.3 km) and take over 6 hours to complete.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about road cycling in Pravia?
A: The road cycling routes in Pravia are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.9 stars from numerous reviews. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain, which combines river valleys, a scenic coastline, and rolling hills, offering engaging experiences for different ability levels.

Q: Are there any routes that combine river valleys with coastal views?
A: Yes, Pravia's unique geography allows for routes that blend these landscapes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33983518" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñaflor Bridge – Santa María del Mar Beach loop from Pravia&lt;/a&gt; is a prime example, combining sections along the river with stunning coastal vistas and significant climbs.
tips: With 18 people in 2011, San Martín de Lodón is a pretty village and parish in the Asturian council of Belmonte de Miranda. If we go through here it is worth going to Ponte, which crosses over the Pigüeña river and offers us great views of it.
Next to the road we find a beautiful suspension bridge that crosses over the Narcea. A good place to take a break if you need it.
10 km from Salas we find this beautiful picture of the village of Soutu los Infantes.
Rolling along the road from Salas to Pravia we pass through El Barrio, which offers us great views of the surrounding area.

waypoints.name: Suspension Bridge over the Narcea River
